headlines.title: U.S.-Iran talks set ‘good foundation’ for deal to end war, Vance says
headlines.description: High-level talks between Iran and the United States resulted in “a lot of good progress,” Vice President JD Vance said as he left Switzerland on Monday after the two sides agreed on a road map toward reaching a final deal to end the war within 60 days.“We laid a very good foundation for a successful final deal,” Vance said at a press briefing before departing for the U.S. “The final deal is the house.

headlines.title: Send your midterm elections questions for Steve Kornacki
headlines.description: Steve Kornacki is getting ready for another night at the Big Board, where he’ll break down primary election results in Maryland, New York, South Carolina and Utah on Tuesday

headlines.title: At least five people arrested in alleged tampering of Reflecting Pool, official says
headlines.description: At least five people have been arrested in connection with the alleged vandalism of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, an administration official said Monday, as President Donald Trump threatened long prison terms for offenders

headlines.title: Supreme Court to weigh rights of federal prison inmates to sue over lack of medical treatment
headlines.description: WASHINGTON — Taking up a case that could further erode the rights of people to sue federal officers for constitutional violations, the Supreme Court on Monday agreed to consider whether a prison inmate could sue a nurse for failing to provide medical assistance after a riot

headlines.title: Influential Former Fed Chair Alan Greenspan Dies at 100
headlines.description: Alan Greenspan, the influential economist who chaired the Federal Reserve for two decades and was the beloved husband of NBC’s Andrea Mitchell, has died at 100 after complications from Parkinson's disease. NBC’s Kristen Welker shares a look back at Greenspan’s life and his impact on the economy.

headlines.title: Trump Says Reflecting Pool Will Likely Need to Be Drained
headlines.description: Despite a recent $14 million renovation, the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool has been plagued with algae blooms that turned the water green and peeling paint. President Donald Trump says the monument will likely need to be at least partly drained for repairs “immediately” and is blaming the problems on vandalism without presenting any evidence. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Embattled UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer Announces Resignation
headlines.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has announced his plans to resign amid mounting pressure from his own party. This comes about two years after Starmer won a massive mandate putting his Labor Party back into power. NBC’s Molly Hunter reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: U.S. deportation tracker: Counting arrests, detentions and border crossings
headlines.description: President Donald Trump came into office promising the largest mass deportation in U.S history, targeting the more than 10 million unauthorized migrants living in the United States.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
headlines.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.

headlines.title: Sen. Cory Booker says people should be ‘very worried’ about Trump and Israel upending peace
headlines.description: Sen. Cory Booker criticized President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, calling them both ‘criminal” leaders.

headlines.title: Trump blames vandals for Reflecting Pool problems and says it may need to be drained again
headlines.description: WASHINGTON — President Donald Trump said Saturday that multiple people had been arrested in connection with alleged vandalism of the Reflecting Pool on the National Mall, including an Olympic canoeist, as the administration’s $14 million project to renovate the site has faced multiple setbacks
